npcs.name: Commander Aldric Vane
npcs.role: Grace-Sworn — military leader of the compact
npcs.description: Thirty years in [[The Grace-Sworn]]. He has kept Hartstone neutral through three crises and does not intend this to be the fourth. He is building options. Some of them are things he can never tell [[The Compact Guild|the Guild]] about.

log.note: Consequence of Lady Ivra Malt (rival): Lady Ivra's arrival — claim presented, forty guards, compact limit exceeded.

[[Commander Aldric Vane]] reads [[Lady Ivra Malt]]'s papers, thanks her, and invites the delegation to guest quarters without confirming the claim. He sends a private rider to [[Brother Edmar]] before dinner.
Consequence of Lady Ivra Malt (rival): Lady Ivra's formal claim — Lord Eddan's documentation presented at the hearing.

[[Commander Aldric Vane]] adjourns the hearing. Asks [[Brother Edmar]] to retrieve the 1187 treaty from the archive. Sends a second rider privately. Does not explain who. His expression does not change at any point during the session.

[[Commander Aldric Vane]] invokes the armed envoy clause: both delegations must reduce guards to twelve within forty-eight hours. [[Lady Ivra Malt]] protests formally. [[Emissary Dax Fell]] immediately complies and offers his excess guards to help patrol the tunnels. [[Commander Aldric Vane|Aldric]] thanks him and does not accept.

npcs.dm_notes: Aldric sent a private rider before dinner on Session 1 — the recipient is a retired Grace-Sworn who knows where the 1187 treaty's original signatories were buried. He's been in contact with this source for three weeks, since before Vespar arrived. He knew this was coming.

npcs.name: Lady Ivra Malt
npcs.role: House Vespar — envoy pressing the northern claim
npcs.description: She arrived with twelve lawyers and forty guards 'for her protection.' She is charming, specific, and never wrong about anything she can cite in writing. She wants Hartstone. She has a fallback position she has not yet revealed. She knows [[Commander Aldric Vane]] better than he would prefer.

log.note: [[Lady Ivra Malt]] presents papers claiming historical title at [[Hartstone North Gate|the north gate]]. Impeccably polite, completely immovable. She thanks [[Commander Aldric Vane]] for the guest quarters before he has finished reading.

[[Lady Ivra Malt]] presents her second claimant's documentation at a formal hearing: Lord Eddan Vespar, third cousin, with a direct treaty right to Hartstone under the Compact of 1187. The records are genuine. [[Emissary Dax Fell]] has a counter. She expected him to.
Consequence of Emissary Dax Fell (rival): Dax Fell's neutrality resolution — dated six months ago, drafted before he arrived in Hartstone.
Consequence of Commander Aldric Vane (rival): Aldric invokes the compact's armed envoy clause — direct enforcement action.

npcs.dm_notes: Ivra and Aldric have met twice before — once at a trade summit seven years ago, once at a border arbitration three years back. She knows he won't bend on the compact. Her fallback: if the claim fails, she will offer Aldric a personal arrangement — Grace-Sworn funding from House Vespar, permanently, in exchange for a 'favorable interpretation.'

npcs.name: Guildmaster Sena Cord
npcs.role: Compact Guild — controls trade and tariff income
npcs.description: [[The Compact Guild|The Guild]] has financed [[The Grace-Sworn]] for two generations. Guildmaster Sena Cord knows this. She is not threatening — she is ensuring everyone understands the math. She will back whoever keeps the trade routes open. She has a price. She has not named it yet.

log.note: [[Guildmaster Sena Cord]] requests a private meeting at [[The Guildhall]]. She confirms [[The Compact Guild]] is aware of the weapons movement — not because the watch told her. She says: 'The compact pays my tariffs. I would prefer it to continue.' She does not name her price. She pours the good wine.
Consequence of Emissary Dax Fell (ally): Dax Fell's neutrality resolution — dated six months ago, drafted before he arrived in Hartstone.
Consequence of Commander Aldric Vane (ally): Aldric invokes the compact's armed envoy clause — direct enforcement action.

npcs.dm_notes: Her price: the Guild wants a permanent seat on the compact's trade arbitration panel — a structural veto over any tariff disputes routed through Hartstone. She has been meeting with Dax Fell for three months. The Kelrath resolution, if it holds, gives the Guild more leverage than either Vespar outcome.

npcs.name: Emissary Dax Fell
npcs.description: He has been in Hartstone for six months before [[House Vespar]] arrived. He knows where every magistrate eats dinner and which ones have debts. He presents as reasonable and collaborative. He is. He is also winning.

log.note: Consequence of Lady Ivra Malt (rival): Lady Ivra's arrival — claim presented, forty guards, compact limit exceeded.
[[Emissary Dax Fell]] dines with [[The Compact Guild]]'s senior merchants the same evening the [[House Vespar]] delegation arrives. He has been here six months. He is not surprised.
Consequence of Lady Ivra Malt (rival): Lady Ivra's formal claim — Lord Eddan's documentation presented at the hearing.

[[Emissary Dax Fell]] produces a ratified [[The Kelrath Envoys|Kelrath]] Council resolution: Hartstone's neutrality supersedes any historical treaty claim. It is dated six months ago. He drafted it before he arrived.

npcs.dm_notes: Dax drafted the Kelrath neutrality resolution while still at the Confederation capital — six months before he arrived. He knew Vespar's play in advance, possibly from an informant inside House Vespar's legal office. [[Osric Fell|Osric]] was at the Academy when the resolution was being drafted. He never saw Dax's name on it until now.

npcs.name: Brother Edmar
npcs.role: Grace-Sworn — chaplain and keeper of founding records
npcs.description: Old enough to remember the last succession crisis. Gentle enough that people tell him things they should not. He spends his evenings in [[The City Archive|the archive]]. He knows something about [[The Underbrick]] he is deciding whether to share.

log.note: [[Brother Edmar]] locates the succession clause in the founding compact: [[The Grace-Sworn]] must recognize the legitimate ruler of any treaty partner. Both [[House Vespar]] claimants have documentation. One set is forged — possibly both. He does not yet know which. He finds something else in the 1187 treaty he is not ready to share.
Consequence of Commander Aldric Vane (ally): Aldric invokes the compact's armed envoy clause — direct enforcement action.

npcs.dm_notes: Brother Edmar has found the 1187 treaty's third provision: a supersession clause that voids both the Vespar dynastic claim and the Kelrath neutrality resolution in the event of 'armed factional presence.' Both delegations are in technical violation. The clause would require the Grace-Sworn to expel both delegations and hold Hartstone independently for one year. He hasn't told Aldric yet because the clause also requires the compact to be financially self-sufficient — which it currently is not.

npcs.name: Petra Wynn
npcs.role: City archivist — custodian of three generations of records
npcs.description: The city's official archivist. She has copies of everything. She does not sell information — she trades it. She knows who owns the old tunnels under the east quarter and what has moved through [[The East Quarter Tunnels|them]] recently. (DM-only until approached)

log.note: Consequence of Emissary Dax Fell (ally): Dax Fell's neutrality resolution — dated six months ago, drafted before he arrived in Hartstone.

npcs.dm_notes: Petra sold the 1187 treaty's third provision to Dax Fell six months ago — before he arrived in Hartstone. That is why his neutrality resolution is so precisely drafted. She didn't know what he'd use it for. She knows now. She also has the tunnel map for the east quarter. She has not offered it to anyone yet.

npcs.name: The Loom
npcs.description: [[The Underbrick]]'s controller. Nobody inside the network has seen their face. They communicate through cutouts and coded drops. They have been buying weapons from both [[House Vespar|Vespar]] and [[The Kelrath Envoys|Kelrath]] suppliers simultaneously. Either they are playing both sides or they are the third side. (DM-only)

log.note: Consequence of Lady Ivra Malt (ally): Lady Ivra's arrival — claim presented, forty guards, compact limit exceeded.
Consequence of Lady Ivra Malt (ally): Lady Ivra's formal claim — Lord Eddan's documentation presented at the hearing.
Consequence of Emissary Dax Fell (ally): Dax Fell's neutrality resolution — dated six months ago, drafted before he arrived in Hartstone.

A cutout is captured in the market. Under questioning: [[The Underbrick]] is being paid by two clients simultaneously for the same weapon delivery — both [[House Vespar|Vespar]] and [[The Kelrath Envoys|Kelrath]] suppliers. [[The Loom]] sold the same shipment twice. One of them is about to realize they did not receive what they paid for.

npcs.dm_notes: The Loom sold the same weapon shipment to both Vespar and Kelrath — not by accident. They are trying to provoke a confrontation between the delegations in Hartstone while the compact's enforcement powers are degraded. In the chaos, the Underbrick becomes the only functioning authority in the east quarter. The Loom is playing a third game, not second fiddle to either delegation.
